PGP in

Format

Full Time
Opt-in Residential

Location

Gurugram
DLF Cyberpark

Commencement Date

June 2026
For Cohort of ‘27

Our PG Programmes Are Designed
For Young Leaders and Experienced Professionals

The PGP TBM YLC is a newly introduced and slightly expanded programme that emphasises on foundational concepts.

PGP TBM

PGP TBM YLC

Duration

16 months

24 months

Eligibility

>1 year of Pre-MBA work ex

0-1 year of Pre-MBA work ex

Number of credits

120 (Inclass + Outclass)

200 (Inclass + Outclass)

Internship

Non mandatory

Mandatory

Terms

8 terms of 1-2 months

8 terms of 3-4 months

Avg. post MBA CTC

₹33.39 L

₹28.24 L

Highest CTC

₹1.28 Cr

₹46.22 L

Cost of programme

₹29.38 L

₹29.38 L

Please note that PGP TBM YLC numbers have been taken from students having 0-1 years of experience from the PGP TBM Cohort

NEW-AGE LEARNING

An MBA Reimagined
From the Ground Up

RELATED LINKS

Curriculum

Download Placement Report

All about admissions

Download Dropshipping Report

SOCIALS

Go Beyond Textbooks
With a Hands-on Curriculum

From launching live projects with top companies to steering a ₹5 cr student-led investment fund, experience hands-on learning like never before.

Learn Business at Real Factories,
Offices, and India’s Biggest Showrooms

From Unicorns like Physics Wallah to the headquarters of fashion brands, study business by spending time "off campus" at India's most celebrated establishments.

Do Consulting Projects
With, Small And Large Businesses

From doing live projects at Microsoft to ideating marketing campaigns for Cars24, BoAt, and Uber, & improving menus for local cafeterias, walk in the shoes of a real-life consultant.

Invest from MU's ₹5 Crore Student Fund

From real estate to startups, from equities to debts, learn investing, not just in theory, but by putting real funds on the line and generating revenue.

Graduate with a Running Business,
Not Just a Job

Launch your startup, pitch it to 100+ investors, and grow your brand as a creator-preneur on Instagram and YouTube. Build, scale, and succeed!

Pitch to 100+ VCs & Angel Investors
At India’s Largest Demo Day

Through Pre-Seed, MVP, GTM, and PMF stages, build your startup step-by-step and strike deals with India’s top VCs.

Explore Business Fundamentals with the Dropshipping Challenge

Students earn over ₹2 crore by building online and offline eCommerce stores with zero inventory!

Become a Creator-preneur on YouTube & Instagram

Learn marketing by building your own influencer brand. Create channels that run into thousands & even millions of followers.

Learn Not Just From Faculty,
But Also Your Future Recruiters

Take courses with top CXOs, MDs, startup founders from prestigious firms like Bain, BCG, & McKinsey, and academicians from Stanford, Wharton & Harvard.

Ace People Management
With Morgan Stanley’s MD

Learn through interactive games, debates, and case studies with our most popular professor of practice, Rajat Mathur.

Master Team Dynamics From
A Renowned Wharton professor

Beyond motivation myths, Dr. Kenwyn K. Smith explores the real drivers of team dynamics , and dives into the essence of leadership.

Power Your EV Career
With Hero Electric’s MD

Naveen Munjal explores the high-voltage opportunities in the electric vehicle sector.

For PGP TBM

Highest CTC

Experience Masters' Union

Get into the shoes of a PGP student, join a roundtable with an alumni, attend an admissions info session in your city, and get all your queries answered by our team.

ALUMNI SUCCESS STORIES

PGP TBM

ALUMNI SUCCESS STORIES

PGP TBM YLC

ALUMNI ACHIEVEMENTS

Hear Straight from Our Alumni

INCUBATOR SUPPORT

Fuel Your Startup Journey With Our Founder Fellowship

The Masters' Union Founder Fellowship is designed to empower serious founders, providing not just financial aid, but a conducive ecosystem for your startup success. Up to 25 serious founders in the PGP Cohort will be awarded ₹40,000 per month to support their startup endeavours for up to a year.

EXPERIENTIAL LEARNING

Witness the Outcomes of
Outclass Curriculum

  • VIP Challenge
  • Case Competitions
  • Creator Challenge
  • Dropshipping Challenge

Abhishek Deb

Senior Associate- BCG

The Masters' Union pedagogy and mentorship program played a crucial role in securing my dream role at BCG.

Mohini Saluja

Assistant Vice President- Citi

The personalised mentorship in resume building, interview prep, career guidance, dropshipping projects, were crucial for my success.

Abhishek Kateliya

Founder’s Office- Strategy Nexstem

StockUp and Nova School were my ventures under the VIP Challenge. This gave me the skills and confidence to build something big.

Priyansh Sharma

Sr. Analyst- Talabaat (Dubai)

I must have met and learned from 50+ CXOs and MDs from top firms on the Masters’ Union campus and that gave me confidence and exposure.

Anurag Bansal

Founder’s Office- Zerodha

The Creator Challenge and Masters' Union Investment Fund set the foundation for my success and set me apart from the rest.

Yeshvant Suresh

Chief of Staff- Ather

I witnessed a massive change in my problem solving ability doing consulting projects and building a startup from scratch.

VIP Challenge

Students dive into an immersive experience of how a business is built from “0 to 1”, understand the various moving parts of a P&L, and create new ventures.

PlaySuper

Raised $600K

An innovative app that seamlessly blends entertainment with tangible shopping rewards, PlaySuper rewards users for their gaming achievements by offering in-app vouchers.

Lexi's

Bootstrapped

This is a fun and quirky cloud kitchen brand making gourmet sandwiches. They aim to build a 1000cr+ food company, starting in DLF Phase 4, Gurgaon, and expanding to more areas soon.

Hive School

Bootstrapped

India’s first sales-focused B-school is set to transform the landscape with its hands-on curriculum that is designed to help students excel in the fast-evolving fields of B2B and SaaS sales.

Woody's Pizzeria

Bootstrapped

Woody’s Pizzeria, a vegetarian-only pizzeria, offers world-class, Indian-inspired Neapolitan and New York pizzas. They've made ₹2.4L monthly , purely organic, with half the menu live, aiming for ₹29L annually.

Mohit Paliwal

Eight.Network

This made-in-India, audio streaming platform has amassed more than 1 million listeners since its inception in 2021.

Eight.Network

Raised $2.08M

This made-in-India audio streaming platform has captivated over 1 million listeners since its launch in 2021, quickly becoming a favourite in the digital audio landscape.

Smit Bhavsar

Heal Paws

Check out this unique product that has been designed to simplify the process of administering medicine or supplements to pets.

Know More

Rishika Kormala

Aikyam

Aikyam offers natural premixes that address chronic gut-health issues, and are specially curated for the Indian lifestyle.

Know More

SeedsAI

Raised $250K

A groundbreaking AI SaaS platform that is pioneering customer relationship intelligence and automation, with solutions to help businesses manage and enhance customer interactions.

Heal Paws

Check out this unique product that has been designed to simplify the process of administering medicine or supplements to pets.

Know More

Aikyam

Aikyam offers natural premixes that address chronic gut-health issues, and are specially curated for the Indian lifestyle.

Know More

Bullspree (Featured on Shark Tank)

Raised $1M+

With 3 lakh+ users, Bullspree has risen the ranks to become one of India’s most popular gaming app that helps users understand stock market dynamics.

Case Competitions

Students participate in case competitions at IIMs & even Harvard etc, and get the opportunity to showcase their strategic thinking .

Governance Challenge

Samagra

Rank 1

Ayushi Gupta, Karan Singh, and Nitya Sharma won the Samagra Governance Challenge 2024 with their innovative Al-driven solutions that impressed an elite panel, including the CM of Uttar Pradesh.

The Ultimate Pitch 10.0

Reliance TUP 10.0

Rank 2

Yashwanth Nandi secured 2nd place at Reliance TUP 10.0 for his climate-smart storage solution, reducing crop loss by 50%.

HUSKify Strategy Case Competition

ISB

Rank 1

Student emerged victorious as national finalists. From cracking a marketing strategy for BigBasket to launching a GTM plan for a US-based food brand, students showcased innovative strategies and out-of-the-box solutions.

Flipkart Wired 8.0 Challenge

Flipkart

National winners

Students competed against top B-schools such as IIMs and ISB, to craft an end-to-end quick commerce entry strategy, complete with an innovative investment thesis and go-to-market execution.

Harvard Case Competition

Harvard Business School

Rank 2

Going head-to-head with esteemed institutions worldwide, including MIT Sloan, Stanford, Columbia, and HEC, students aced a case competition examining Merger & Acquisition cases.

IIM Ahmedabad's Brain-A-Thon

IIM Ahmedabad

Rank 1

A unique marketing case study challenge where students collaborated with a firm in the meat-alternatives industry to overhaul their brand strategy, aiming to boost revenue.

IIM Bangalore’s Imagineer Voyage

IIM Bangalore

Rank 1

Students showcased their skills in idea innovation, product management, and strategy, to craft comprehensive business models and bring their products to market.

IIM Calcutta’s Prodigy

IIM Calcutta

Rank 1

A GTM case challenge where students created innovative product concepts, and defined market entry tactics for a new company in the health-monitoring device market.

Samay-Sya '24

Ashoka University

Rank 2

Manan did an in-depth competitor analysis which included factors like founder equity stability, manufacturing location and even financial projections, clearly highlighting Noise' competitive edge.

Mastering the case: Consulting Strategy

IIT Kanpur

Rank 1

UG Class of 2027 students Sai Vishnu, Sabarna Jana, and Anish Admane won 1st place by applying concepts from the Building Marketing Strategies course, like STP Analysis and Customer Inertia.

Case Competition

St. Xavier's College

Rank 2

UG Class of 2027 students Bhargav and Yash secured 2nd place among top teams from SRCC, Hansraj, and Ashoka for their bold profit-maximizing approach to an amusement park launch.

Our aim is to become Top 10 B-School!

Students crafted Product Development and Go-To-Market (GTM) strategy

Microsoft Live Project

Our students are on an exhilarating journey with Microsoft through a live project and get hands-on tech experience.

Ather Energy Live Project

Masters' Union students formulate the pricing strategy for a cutting-edge innovation enhancing Ather’s features.

Creator Challenge

Students transform their passion into profit by mastering the art of building and monetizing personal brands as Creator-preneurs.

Rajas Gupta

@rajas_guptaa

1486 followers

A daily dose of heartwarming, relatable, and feel-good slice-of-life content.

DinCharya Diaries

@DinCharyaDiaries

286 subscribers

A popular channel capturing the raw, and beautifully imperfect moments of everyday life.

Ishan Godha

@bro_knows_a_lot

184 followers

Simplifying the world of successful brands, emerging trends, and global headlines to keep you ahead of the curve.

Isheta Agrawal

@isheta_agrawal

11.2K followers

Step into the world of style and sophistication with a creator who brings fashion and lifestyle to life!

Dibyansu Mohanty

@actually.dibs

1431 followers

A digital creator at the intersection of AI and gaming, exploring the latest innovations and trends.

Dhruvi Chhajer

@adventureswithdhruvi

459 followers

A daily dose of humor, straight from the heart of life’s little absurdities.

Business with Bansal

181K followers

A popular channel creating content on business, marketing, and finance strategies of top companies.

Medium Masala

42.8K followers

The go-to channel for latest updates on the business world, decoding lives of influencers and CEOs.

Chandrayee Bose

54.1K followers

A fun dose of GRWM videos, snippets about life, and everything about fashion and lifestyle.

The Humming Snitch

7K followers

Comics, doodles and other art forms abound in this channel that talks about daily life musings.

Series Maddy

2.7K followers

This channel shares dialogues with Founders, VCs, and CXOs, providing a look into different careers.

Dropshipping Challenge

Students target to get 10 lakh in revenue (and more) by building their eCommerce ventures & selling on Amazon, BlinkIt & own websites.

Download Report

Zeller Crystals

₹14.17 lakh revenue

Born with the vision to create India's first crystal couture brand, this company promises to offer the most stylish showpieces, chandeliers, and more.

The Frenzie Store

₹14 lakh revenue

The go-to destination for everyone’s hair care needs, this brand offers products that transform your mane into frizz-free, shiny, and smooth perfection.

Khareedo.shop

₹11.8 lakh revenue

From cutting-edge drones, stylish home decor, to orthopaedic pillows, and stress-busting balls, this dropshipping business has something for everyone.

ThriftFly

₹11.3 lakh revenue

Thrift Fly is your ultimate travel companion, helping you score discounts on all your travel plans, whether you're booking flights, hotels, or vacation packages.

STUDENT JOURNEY

Life at Masters' Union

Get to know our alumni better and understand how PGP help

Explore Student Projects

Explore Student Projects

Experience the excellence of our student community – a glimpse into what you can achieve here.

Learn More
Engage with Clubs

Engage with Clubs

Discover your passion and forge lifelong connections. Know more on how our clubs work and how you can participate.

Learn More
Dive into Entrepreneurship

Dive into Entrepreneurship

Discover how you can learn business by running a business in real-time.

Learn More

Go Global with Masters’ Union’s Unique International Immersions

Interact with global C-suite leaders during industrial visits and workshops in cities across the world, and participate in classes at prestigious B-schools.

Immerse at World’s Top Ranked B-Schools

Study abroad at prestigious global B-schools such as INSEAD, Bocconi, NUS, Babson, and gain unmatched international insights.

Exhibit at the World's Largest Trade Event- China's Canton Fair

Visit the epicentre of global trade, meet 10 lakh+ customers and suppliers from more than 150 countries, and strike deals to increase your profitability.

Spend a Term Abroad in Dubai

Spend a term abroad in Dubai, immersing yourself in one of the world's most dynamic business hubs, and expanding your global network.

Experience Learning via National & International Immersions

Our Immersion Programs offer students hands-on exposure to leading business practices in India and around the world. Structured around thematic tracks, students choose their focus area and gain industry experience, real-world insights, and cultural fluency.

Global immersions

Step Into the World’s Most Dynamic
at Markets and Classrooms

  • Courses taught in collaboration with top brands like KPMG, Schbang , Cars24, etc.

  • Work on live projects, shadow CEOs, and even manage an investment fund.

INSEAD (Paris)

Learn how strategy meets innovation through 4 sessions on Blue Ocean Strategy, 3 on sustainable innovation, and 3 on stakeholder engagement along with company visits to Schneider Electric, Rungis Market, and leading Paris-based startups.

NUS (Singapore)

Dive into Singapore’s startup ecosystem through 10 sessions on entrepreneurship and venture capital, 5 on blockchain and digital finance, and 5 on AI and innovation, complemented by company visits and simulation exercises.

Babson College (USA)

Discover how ventures grow from idea to impact through sessions on entrepreneurship, innovation, and leadership, with company visits to Boston’s leading startups and accelerators.

SDA Bocconi (Italy)

Gain a global perspective through 6 sessions on strategy and decision-making, 4 on innovation and tech, and 4 on sustainability. Company visits to Dallara Automobili and a balsamic vinegar producer in Modena.

NBS (Singapore)

Master the dynamics of venture capital and AI-driven business through 2 sessions on growth strategies and 3 on AI and business, complemented by company visits to leading Singapore-based enterprises.

WBS (United Kingdom)

Discover the future of sustainable business through 3 sessions on sustainability and ESG, 2 on innovation and design thinking, and 1 on creative leadership, plus company visits to Morgan Motors and leading UK enterprises.

ESCP (Germany)

Experience Berlin’s vibrant innovation landscape through 3 sessions on entrepreneurship, 2 on digital marketing, and 2 on mobility and smart industries, with exclusive company visits to Sony, BMW Motorradwerke, and MotionLab Berlin.

Ivey (Canada)

Explore Canada’s thriving innovation ecosystem through 5 sessions on commercialization, innovation and leadership, entrepreneurial ecosystems, and corporate entrepreneurship, plus company visits to Schmeichel London Business and Communitech Waterloo.

Foster School of Business (USA)

Learn the art of AI-led transformation through 2 sessions on AI and technology, 2 on automation and markets, and 1 on leadership, plus company visits to Boeing and Starbucks and a case challenge.

Step into the World of
Global Business

  • Courses taught in collaboration with top brands like KPMG, Schbang , Cars24, etc.

  • Work on live projects, shadow CEOs, and even manage an investment fund.

Exhibit at the World's Largest Trade Event- China's Canton Fair

Visit the epicentre of global trade, meet 10 lakh+ customers and suppliers from more than 150 countries, and strike deals to increase your profitability.

Explore Global Giants in the GCC Region

Discover how Fortune 500 companies and start-ups are reshaping the GCC region. Visit giants like Emirates and Aramco, and engage with executives driving innovation.

national immersions

With the Bharat Immersion, Spend a
Full Term Traveling Across India’s
Business Hubs

We believe that the best classrooms have no walls and with our Bharat Immersion, you’ll spend Term 3 traveling across India, meeting entrepreneurs, factories, and markets that move the nation. You earn credits as you learn how business is done on the ground.

7,000 km, 20+ Cities.
One mission – to learn how India
does business

From Chandni Chowk in Delhi to Adani Enterprises in Ahmedabad. From
Dharma Productions in Mumbai to the distilleries in Goa – this is
business education, reimagined.

Hub 1

Delhi NCR

Visit Chandni Chowk to trace retail heritage, explore D2C brands, experience Parliament, ECI, NDMA and attend 9 CXO sessions.

Hub 2

Jalandhar

Uncover how ITC manufactures, how NIVIA went global, explore agriculture manufacturing at Sonalika, attend 5 CXO sessions and witness large-scale education at LPU.

Hub 3

Mundra & Ahmedabad

Explore 3 Adani enterprises covering Ports, Wilmar, and Solar, tour Amul's dairy factory, engage with IIM Ahmedabad Ventures, and attend 4 CXO sessions.

Hub 4

Mumbai

Unravel what goes behind iconic names like Dharma, the Dabbawalas and Godrej, gain financial insights from RBI, BSE/NSE, and HDFC and attend CXO sessions.

Hub 5

Goa

Cruise to Goa on Cordelia, explore iron mines and a Feni distillery, and immerse in Fado music, Portuguese culture, and Goa's nightlife.

Hub 6

Bengaluru

Learn to build winning e-commerce strategies, explore aerospace innovation and understand how IT services became India's largest export success.

Hub 7

Darjeeling

Experience a rural immersion in Indo-Nepal villages and visit Darjeeling tea estates to learn brand building, culture, and sustainability.

Hub 8

Lucknow

Explore Uttar Pradesh's defense manufacturing, global luxury embroidery, and meet government leaders shaping a trillion-dollar economy.

The immersions listed are indicative and may change based on partner availability and unforeseen circumstances.

7,000 km, 20+ Cities.
One mission – to learn how India
does business

From Chandni Chowk in Delhi to Adani Enterprises in Ahmedabad. From
Dharma Productions in Mumbai to the distilleries in Goa – this is
business education, reimagined.

The immersions listed are indicative and may change based on partner availability and unforeseen circumstances.

OFF CAMPUS

1 Day Immersions

Business management meets hands-on experience, giving you real-world
skills that truly stand out.

CARS24

Experience how Cars24 is transforming India’s used-car market through data, technology, and customer trust

ShipRocket

Step inside India’s leading logistics tech startup powering the D2C revolution and learn how innovation fuels e-commerce growth at scale.

Nivia

Witness how Nivia built an Indian sports brand competing globally through innovation in manufacturing, branding, and grassroots engagement.

HANDS-ON & INDUSTRY-DRIVEN

Visit International Trade Fairs to Learn the Business of
Manufacturing, Supply-Chains, and Global Commerce

Witness the Family Business Lab China’s Canton Fair Network in the GCC Region

Witness the Family Business Lab

50% of the entire PGP in Family Business Management is about fixing your own family business. Each term, you will work on one capstone project with your own family business team, aided by professional facilitators. You will identify challenges and goals, creating actionable plans tailored to your family enterprise.

Exhibit at the World's Largest Trade Event-China's Canton Fair

Visit the epicentre of global trade, meet 10 lakh+ customers and suppliers from more than 150 countries, and strike deals to increase your profitability.

Explore Global Giants in the GCC Region

Discover how Fortune 500 companies and start-ups are reshaping the GCC region. Visit giants like Emirates and Aramco, and engage with executives driving innovation.

Visit the epicentre of global trade, meet 10 lakh+ customers and suppliers from more than 150 countries, and strike deals to increase your profitability.

Each term for experienced professionals lasts 1-2 months and 2-3 months for freshers.

Go Global with Masters’ Union’s Unique International Immersions

Interact with global C-suite leaders during industrial visits and workshops in cities across the world, and participate in classes at prestigious B-schools.

Immerse at World’s Top Ranked B-Schools

Study abroad at prestigious global B-schools such as INSEAD and Bocconi and gain unmatched international insights.

Get Inside Most Iconic International Brands

From Japan’s Toyota to Italy’s Ferrari, visit top international brands and gain first hand exposure to business practices.

Build Your Network & Crack Jobs Abroad

The international exposure equips you to crack jobs abroad in countries like Ireland, UAE & even USA.

A Glimpse Into
Your Weekly Schedule

MASTERS OF THE INDUSTRY

Learn Directly from Future Recruiters

Take courses with CXOs and MDs from top companies, startup founders, and academicians from renowned global institutes.

Learn tech, business, and related core skills from highly-rated and experienced Professors-in-Residence.

Get 1-1 mentorship from Masters in your preferred domain.

The Masters’ Union Advantage

Gain insights from our PGP TBM Director, Mahak Garg, as she dives into the dynamic and enriching experiences that the PGP TBM programme offers. Discover what’s in store for students who plan to study at Masters’ Union!

ENROLLMENT CHECKLIST

Admission Criteria

01.

Internships and Work Experience

01.

Internships and Work Experience

For the PGP TBM, applicants usually have 2–5 years of experience, though exceptional candidates outside this range are welcome.
For the PGP TBM Young Leaders Cohort (YLC), candidates with 0–1 year of experience or in their final undergraduate year are eligible. Relevant internships and a clear connection between past experiences and future goals enhance your application.

  • For the PGP TBM: Applicants must mandatorily have a minimum of one year pre-MBA work experience.

  • For the PGP TBM Young Leaders Cohort: Candidates with 0–1 year of experience or in their final year of graduation are eligible. Internships will hold a high weightage in the young leaders cohort.

Weightage

~33%

02.

Academics & Co-Curricular

02.

Academics & Co-Curricular

We place equal importance on both academics and co-curricular activities. Academic performance is evaluated through grades in graduation and post-graduation courses. We also value advanced degrees or certifications. Applicants are encouraged to submit their CAT, GMAT and GMAT Focus scores. However, this is not mandatory.

Weightage

~33%

03.

Communication

03.

Communication

All candidates will be assessed throughout the application process on their ability to structure their thoughts. We evaluate this in both verbal and written formats. This comprehensive assessment is essential for success in the programme and in the business world.

Weightage

~33%

JOIN THE PG PROGRAMME

Application
Timeline and Process
(PGP TBM and PGP TBM YLC)

Final Round Application Deadline MU-BAAT Deadline Personal Interview
*(Dates may vary)		 Application Fee Admission Fee Deadline Status
Round 1 12th October 2025 19th October 2025 28th October 2025 ₹500/- (Early bird) Payable as per 'date of payment', specified in offer letter Closed
Round 2 28th November 2025 7th December 2025 13th December 2025 ₹1,000/- Payable as per 'date of payment', specified in offer letter Apply now Closed
Round 3 11th January 2026 18th January 2026 26th January 2026 ₹1,500/- Payable as per 'date of payment', specified in offer letter Apply now Closed
Round 4 1st March 2026 8th March 2026 16th March 2026 ₹1,500/- Payable as per 'date of payment', specified in offer letter Apply now
Final Evaluation Window 3rd May 2026 7th May 2026 10th May 2026 ₹1,500/- Payable as per 'date of payment', specified in offer letter Apply now

The batch commences in June 2025.

JOIN THE PG PROGRAMME

Application
Timeline and Process (For PGP TBM)

Rounds Application Deadline Video Based Response (VBR) Deadline Personal Interview *
(Dates may vary)		 Application Fee Admission Fee Deadline Status

Closed
Round 2 2nd February 2025 9th February 2025 19th February - 3rd March 2025 ₹3,500/- Payable as per 'date of payment', specified in offer letter

The batch commences in June 2025.

01.

Complete the Application Form

01.

Complete The Application Form

Requirements

Fill out your details

You will need:

  • Your latest resume - Applicants are allowed to choose their own format (needs to be clear and concise). Feel free to use our sample resume format.

  • Your qualification documents - Upload all documents related to your work experience, academics and extra-curriculars to support your application.

Upload your CAT/GMAT/GMAT Focus score

You may submit your CAT/GMAT/GMAT Focus scores in your application.

Video Based Response (VBR)

All applicants will be required to submit a short video based on a prompt that will be given to you on the spot. This will help us assess your communication skills, with a focus on clear and structured thinking. You don’t need extensive preparation — just show up as your true self.

  • Preparation Time: 1 minute

  • Video Duration: Approximately 2-4 minutes

02.

MU-BAAT (Masters’ Union Business Aptitude and Admissions Test)

02.

MU-BAAT (Masters’ Union Business Aptitude and Admissions Test)

Requirements

Applicants who do not have a valid CAT or GMAT score (or if you feel your score does not reflect your true potential) are required to take the MU-BAAT. If you have submitted a CAT or GMAT score and are happy with it, then you are not required to take the MU-BAAT.

The MU-BAAT is designed to evaluate:

  • Your communication and business aptitude

  • Clarity of thought and idea structuring

  • Creative and analytical thinking

You record your responses online and do not need any prior preparation. Think of the questions as a conversation with the Masters’ Union team that helps us understand you beyond your résumé.

Duration: 45-60 minutes

Format: 9 questions divided into 5 sections. The test starts with a simple, fun question and gradually moves to questions focused on your story, motivation, creativity, and analytical thinking. You will receive short breaks between sections.

Test Window:

After you submit your application and pay the application fee, our admissions team will review your profile. The MU-BAAT test link will be shared on the day following the application deadline. Once you receive the link, you will be required to complete the test within the test window communicated in the email.

Sample Questions:

  • If you could shadow any business leader for one day, who would it be and what would you aim to learn?

  • A D2C skincare brand increased their ad spend, but average order value fell 20%, while new users grew. What do you think happened?

02.

Masters’ Union
AI Assessment and Interaction

02.

Masters’ Union AI Assessment and Interaction

About the Interview

This is only required for applicants who do not submit their GRE/GMAT/CAT scores.

The video-based AI Assessment and Interaction represents our commitment to staying at the forefront of technology, offering a cutting-edge and unbiased admissions experience. It will be available for 7 days starting the day after the application round deadline.

This interaction will focus purely on assessing your critical thinking skills, and what you can bring to Masters' Union. It requires no preparation - just show up as your true self.

03.

Personal Interview

03.

Personal Interview

First Shortlist and Interview Scheduling

Following a comprehensive evaluation, shortlisted applicants will receive an invitation to proceed to the interview stage.

  • If selected, applicants will be informed of the interview date, venue, and time 3–5 days prior to the interview.

  • The interviews will be conducted by distinguished faculty members and seasoned industry leaders.

04.

Final Admission Decision

04.

Final Admission Decision

Status of evaluation

Based on the final decision of the admissions committee, applicants will be slotted into the
following categories-

  • Applicants who have been offered admission to Masters’ Union will receive their decision along with the financial structure for the upcoming programme.

  • For applicants who are waitlisted, the admissions committee will reconvene and promote candidates from the waitlisted to the accepted pool, on a regular basis. Please note that we do not maintain a ranked waitlist. Your admission is dependent on the spots that might open up later.

  • If you’ve received a rejection email, you cannot apply in any subsequent round in the same academic year. You are welcome to re-apply to the programme in the next academic year.

Getting Into Masters' Union

Dive into this exclusive Q&A session where our admissions team addresses all your questions about the process. From eligibility criteria to application tips, and from interview preparation to life on campus-get all the insights you need to make an informed decision.

Scholarship Opportunities

Investing in the Future of Our Students

100%

Maximum merit-based scholarship received by a student.

50+

Scholarships available for the postgraduate batch.

100%

Loan coverage for the programme and hostel fees.

  • Manoj Kohli Merit-based Scholarship
  • Rajni Thethi Scholarship for Women
  • Chairman’s Diversity Scholarship
  • MU Scholarship for Technology Leaders
  • P. Dwarakanath India-bound Scholarship
  • Pankaj Bansal Scholarship for Young Leaders
  • Founders’ Bursary for Entrepreneurs
  • Siblings Scholarship
  • Bharat Innovators Scholarship

    • Manoj Kohli Merit-based Scholarship

    The Manoj Kohli Merit-based Scholarship extends financial support to candidates with exceptional academic records and professional accomplishments. These scholarships are offered as partial waivers of the tuition fee, as decided by the admissions committee.

    Rajni Thethi Scholarship for Women

    The Rajni Thethi Scholarship for Women extends support to deserving female candidates with impressive academic and/or professional track record, along with the drive and potential to emerge as an industry leader.

    Chairman’s Diversity Scholarship

    The Chairman’s Diversity Scholarship aims at the inclusion of exceptional candidates from marginalised and underserved communities from the Indian subcontinent, such as the Northeast, Ladakh, Bhutan, and the tribal belts of the Indian hinterland.

    MU Scholarship for Technology Leaders

    The MU Scholarship for Technology Leaders is for candidates who have demonstrated technology-oriented leadership either as students or in their jobs, through innovations, patents or research, and are looking to further their career prospects in the technology domain.

    P. Dwarakanath India-bound Scholarship

    The P. Dwarakanath India-bound Scholarship is directed at Indian-origin candidates (i.e. PIO card-holders) who wish to engage with the Indian corporate sector, as well as Indian professionals currently based abroad, who wish to return to India to pursue world-class education and catapult their careers to newer heights.

    Pankaj Bansal Scholarship for Young Leaders

    The Pankaj Bansal Scholarship for Young Leaders aims to identify young professionals who have displayed exceptional drive and leadership potential through their professional projects, community service and active participation in student-led committees, clubs, and other initiatives while in college.

    Founder’s Bursary for Entrepreneurs

    The Founders’ Bursary for Entrepreneurs funds candidates who have had entrepreneurial experience, with varying levels of success, and wish to gain more skills and academic perspectives to carry on with their line of work.

    Siblings Scholarship

    The Siblings Scholarship offers financial support to candidates who have siblings in the current or previous cohorts of Masters’ Union. This scholarship recognises the continued trust placed in us by families and celebrates their shared journey within the Masters’ Union community. Awards are offered as partial waivers of the tuition fee, as decided by the admissions committee.

    Bharat Innovators Scholarship

    The Bharat Innovators Scholarship is aimed at young Indian entrepreneurs from non-metropolitan towns and villages who have launched a startup or built a minimum viable product (MVP). This scholarship supports grassroots innovators looking to scale their ventures while gaining access to world-class education, mentorship, and a thriving startup ecosystem.

    Manoj Kohli Merit-Based Scholarship

    The Manoj Kohli Merit-based Scholarship extends financial support to candidates with exceptional academic records and professional accomplishments. These scholarships are offered as partial waivers of the tuition fee, as decided by the admissions committee.

    Rajni Thethi Scholarship for Women

    The Rajni Thethi Scholarship for Women extends support to deserving female candidates with impressive academic and/or professional track record, along with the drive and potential to emerge as an industry leader.

    Chairman’s Diversity Scholarship

    The Chairman’s Diversity Scholarship aims at the inclusion of exceptional candidates from marginalised and underserved communities from the Indian subcontinent, such as the Northeast, Ladakh, Bhutan, and the tribal belts of the Indian hinterland.

    MU Scholarship For Technology Leaders

    The MU Scholarship for Technology Leaders is for candidates who have demonstrated technology-oriented leadership either as students or in their jobs, through innovations, patents or research, and are looking to further their career prospects in the technology domain.

    P. Dwarakanath India-bound Scholarship

    The P. Dwarakanath India-bound Scholarship is directed at Indian-origin candidates (i.e. PIO card-holders) who wish to engage with the Indian corporate sector, as well as Indian professionals currently based abroad, who wish to return to India in pursuit of a world-class education, that may help catapult their careers to newer heights.

    Pankaj Bansal Scholarship For Young Leaders

    The Pankaj Bansal Scholarship for Young Leaders aims to identify young professionals who have displayed exceptional drive and leadership potential through their professional projects, community service and active participation in student-led committees, clubs, and other initiatives while in college.

    Founders’ Bursary For Entrepreneurs

    The Founders’ Bursary for Entrepreneurs funds candidates who have had entrepreneurial experience, with varying levels of success, and wish to gain more skills and academic perspectives to carry on with their line of work.

    Siblings Scholarship

    The Siblings Scholarship offers financial support to candidates who have siblings in the current or previous cohorts of Masters’ Union. This scholarship recognises the continued trust placed in us by families and celebrates their shared journey within the Masters’ Union community. Awards are offered as partial waivers of the tuition fee, as decided by the admissions committee.

    Bharat Innovators Scholarship

    The Bharat Innovators Scholarship is aimed at young Indian entrepreneurs from non-metropolitan towns and villages who have launched a startup or built a minimum viable product (MVP). This scholarship supports grassroots innovators looking to scale their ventures while gaining access to world-class education, mentorship, and a thriving startup ecosystem.

    Student Success Stories

    • Placements
    • Startups

    Shreya Kulkarni

    Product Manager II, Microsoft

    Mentors like Aditya Turalapati (PM2, Microsoft) and Sumit Kumar provided vital guidance, while the PM curriculum, live projects, and global placement approach thoroughly prepared me.

    Krishna Goyal

    Product Manager, Cisco

    The VIP challenge at Masters’ Union laid the foundation for my success in securing multiple job offers and transitioning from Marketing to Product Management. Building a product firsthand taught me more than any class.

    Deep Bhatia

    Investment Banking Associate, Axis Capital

    The support at Masters’ Union was incredible—faculty, the career prep team, and batchmates made the placement journey seamless. Mock interviews honed my technical skills for Investment Banking roles.

    Priyal Jain

    Management Consultant, Accenture

    Masters' Union sharpened my problem-solving skills, while mentors and the Consulting Garage provided real-world insights for future challenges.

    CA Harsh Nahar

    Senior Business Analyst, Kearney

    From being elected Vice President to organizing Case Union, India’s largest case competition conclave, the experience was filled with rewarding challenges.

    Radhika Agarwal

    Investment Analyst, Good Capital (VC)

    Masters' Union’s support and career prep made me placement-ready, shaping my journey to becoming an Investment Analyst at Good Capital.

    Shruti Kumari

    Manager - Brand Marketing, Flipkart

    Masters’ Union provided me with unparalleled exposure to the e-commerce landscape, which significantly shaped my understanding of the sector.

    Prabhav Narang

    Associate, EY-P

    Masters' Union sharpened my analytical skills, while real-world projects built my confidence for financial analytics at EY-P.

    Kakaraparthi Sri Badarinadh

    Senior Specialist- Strategic Accounts, Talabat (Dubai)

    Masters' Union's case-based teaching was key to my interview success, helping me approach challenges with a structured mindset.

    Bhavneet Chandi

    Associate Program Manager, Zomato

    Masters’ Union shaped my career, equipping me with a strategic mindset through real-world projects for success at Zomato's strategy team.

    Tilottama Ghosh

    Associate, Kotak Investment Bank

    The focus on financial modeling and investment strategies built my foundation, while industry mentorship sharpened my problem-solving skills.

    Vedang Lahoti

    Senior Manager I-Category, Zepto

    Industry mentors and real-world case studies equipped me to navigate complexities confidently as Senior Category Manager at Zepto.

    Bullspree

    Featured on Shark Tank, 3 lakh + users

    Bullspree is an experiential stock market gaming platform that empowers users to understand stock market dynamics through interactive games.

    Eight.Network

    Raised $2.08 million over 2 funding rounds

    This made-in-India, audio streaming platform has amassed more than 1 million listeners since its inception in 2021.

    Buzzinga

    A first-of-its-kind snack crisps

    Buzzinga created all the right buzz with its ready-to-serve, non-vegetarian snacking crisps, garnering 40,000+ orders since its launch.

    Aikyam

    A powerful, digestive health solution

    Aikyam offers natural premixes that address chronic gut-health issues, and are specially curated for the Indian lifestyle.

    Crispee

    Unique dosa chips with 10,000+ orders

    This affordable and healthy fusion snacking option is taking India by storm, one unique flavour at a time.

    73 Boston

    Tea-infused wine, with revenue of ₹7.5 lakh

    73 Boston boasts of being selected as one of 9 startups in India to join GradCapital accelerator program, a VC fund for student startups.

    Soul Gappa

    A high gross margin of 75%

    Soul Gappa is revolutionising India’s beloved street food - gol gappa, making it hygienic & also uniquely tailored for Indian regions.

    TailorHub

    A platform for your style journey

    TailorHub is a centralised platform to connect customers with their nearest tailors, and solve for the hassles of finding your perfect fit.

    Heal Paws

    Oral strips for your pet’s wellness

    Check out this unique product that has been designed to simplify the process of administering medicine or supplements to pets.

    Careers Team

    Reach out to our team at careerservices@mastersunion.org

    ANNUAL ROADMAP

    Benefit From a Tailored Career Pathway

    Leverage the opportunity to engage in workshops, training, panel discussions, counselling sessions, and personalised career progression plans.

    • Term 1
    • Term 2
    • Term 3
    • Term 4
    • Term 5
    • Term 6
    • Term 7
    • Term 8

    Mentor Allocation & Profile Building

    • Psychometric Assessment

    • Professional Photography & Class Photos
    • Institution of PlaCom
    • Know all Domains
    • Placement Policies
    • Case and Business Plan Competition Workshop

    Outreach Plan & Personal Branding

    • Finalisation of Company Lists
    • Company Outreach Plan
    • Shortlisting Your Top 3 Domains
    • Presentation Skills Workshop
    • Industry Engagement Sessions
    • Personal Branding Workshop
    • Storytelling Workshop
    • Career Practicums
    • Corporate Competitions

    • Resume Review #1
    • Behavioural Mocks
    • Consulting Case Training
    • Domain-Specific Seminars and Workshops

    • Industry/Functional/Role Based Workshops

    Interview Preparations

    • Resume Review #2
    • Stress Management Sessions
    • Domain-Specific Seminars
    • Corporate Competitions
    • Industry/Functional/Role Based Workshops
    • Mock Interviews

    QUERIES

    Frequently Asked
    Questions

    • General
    • Eligibility
    • Application Queries
    • Entrance and Essays
    • Admission Policies
    • Immersions

    What is a typical day of a Masters’ Union student?

    At Masters’ Union, our dynamic and hands-on pedagogy ensures that each day offers a unique learning experience. With 2-3 classes per day, each spanning 120 minutes, students delve deep into diverse business topics, engaging with expert faculty and their peers in stimulating discussions and practical exercises. Beyond classes, students immerse themselves in a variety of enriching activities throughout the week- CXO Sessions, Industry Practicums, Career Counselling Sessions etc.

    Can applicants tailor their Masters’ Union PGP TBM?

    Masters’ Union offers more than 87 electives, giving students the chance to construct a tailor-made postgraduate program that fits their personal and professional goals. We have new-age, core, and industry concentrations. Students are free to pursue a generalist path toward management or specialise in particular areas by choosing industry concentrations. Regardless of the path students decide to take during their elective terms, everybody graduates with the same PGP TBM certificate with an opportunity to choose industry concentrations.

    What is the difference between the Flagship PGP TBM program vs the PGP TBM YLC?

    The content and pedagogy of both PGP TBM and PGP TBM YLC remain identical. The PGP TBM Young Leaders Cohort is specifically designed for individuals with 0–1 year of pre-MBA experience. It follows the same curriculum as the PGP TBM but spans 24 months instead of 16 months. A key highlight of the PGP TBM YLC is a mandatory 3-month internship, offering students practical, real-world exposure to prepare them for leadership roles. Additionally, each core course is taught over 30 hours instead of 20 hours in the PGP TBM. This ensures that students build a strong foundation in critical academic concepts.

    Are the Scholarships the Same for PGP TBM and PGP TBM YLC?

    Yes, the scholarships available for both the PGP TBM and the PGP TBM YLC are the same. For detailed information on the scholarship options, please refer to our admissions page.

    How will applicants be graded at the Masters’ Union?

    The final grades for most courses will be determined by a mix of class participation, quizzes, simulations, group assignments, projects and exams. Masters’ Union grades the exams on a Standard Normal Curve, also called a Z-curve, with a mean of 3.0. There are no forced-to-fails. To graduate, students must maintain a mean grade of 2.1 in both the core and electives courses.

    What are the benefits of the 16-month programme?

    The PGP TBM at Masters’ Union, with its unique 16-month format, encapsulates the best global practices in management education through a pedagogical approach. Designed to be a fast-paced academic experience, it includes 13 months of rigorous academic training in core areas and industry concentrations with a three-month period of internship and industry immersion, and opportunities to network and seek mentorship. The course is designed for individuals with more than 1 year of work experience.Being in this state of constant engagement gives an opportunity for students to learn how to set priorities and make choices about learning, socialising and personal time from the very beginning of the programme, making them an expert at decision-making, which is one of the most valued qualities in a business leader.

    What are the benefits of the 24-month PGP TBM YLC programme?

    The PGP TBM YLC, on the other hand, is specifically crafted for individuals with 0–1 year of pre-MBA experience and spans 24 months. While the content and pedagogy are identical to the flagship program, the extended timeline allows for deeper learning and gradual development. Each core course in the PGP TBM YLC is delivered over 30 hours instead of 20, ensuring students build a solid foundation in critical concepts. A key feature of this program is the mandatory three-month internship, which provides students with hands-on industry experience to prepare them for leadership roles. With the additional time and focus on practical exposure, the PGP TBM YLC offers an enriching pathway for early-stage professionals to kickstart their careers with confidence.

    If an applicant is from a non-tech background, is the Masters’ Union a right fit for them?

    Both the 16-month PGP TBM and the 24-month PGP TBM YLC are designed to seamlessly integrate students from diverse backgrounds, including non-tech fields.While the programs introduce concepts like programming and software design, they remain approachable for those without prior technical expertise. Technology is just one of many skill sets students acquire, alongside business, design, and marketing. Electives allow students to customize their learning, choosing tech-focused courses or exploring other areas like finance, marketing, or entrepreneurship.With practical learning, mentorship, and flexibility, Masters’ Union ensures all students, regardless of their background, are prepared for leadership roles in a dynamic business world.

    What is the typical student's profile at Masters’ Union?

    Masters’ Union doesn’t have a ‘typical’ student profile. Both the PGP TBM and PGP TBM YLC attract individuals from diverse academic, professional, and geographical backgrounds.The PGP TBM typically includes professionals with more than 1 year of work experience, while the PGP TBM YLC is tailored for recent graduates or those with 0–1 year of experience. This diversity enriches peer learning and creates a dynamic classroom environment.

    If an applicant just completed/will be completing their undergraduate studies, is Masters’ Union a right fit for them?

    Work experience is not a mandatory requirement for admission to the PGP TBM YLC. However, for the PGP TBM you must have a minimum 1 year of work experience. All those who will be completing their undergrad degree by May of the following year will be eligible to apply.

    How many years of work experience does an applicant need to be admitted?

    For the PGP TBM program, applicants typically need a minimum of 2 years of work experience to gain the most from its fast-paced and immersive curriculum. However, work experience is not mandatory for admission to the PGP TBM YLC, which is designed specifically for freshers and individuals with up to 1 year of experience.

    What's the minimum qualifying GPA?

    Masters’ Union does not have a minimum GPA requirement as the GPA system is not a global grading methodology. We focus on the courses students have taken, grades received and overall academic performance.

    Is there an age limit for entry?

    There is no strict age limit for entry into Masters’ Union programs. However, the eligibility varies based on work experience:   For the PGP TBM Young Leaders Cohort (PGP TBM YLC), applicants should have 0–1 year of experience. For the PGP TBM, applicants are expected to have more than 1 year of work experience. Individuals with more than 10 years of work experience are encouraged to contact the admissions team at admissions@mastersunion.org for a personalized evaluation and guidance on their application.

    If an applicant doesn't have a business background, will this impact their chances for admission?

    We value diversity at every level and welcome applications from a variety of professional and academic backgrounds, including the arts, law, medicine, military, and non-profit. As with all candidates, we will be looking for demonstrable leadership potential and will consider this in conjunction with their professional experience and past performance when assessing their application.

    If an applicant doesn’t have an undergraduate degree, can they still be considered for admission?

    Yes! While the vast majority of our participants have undergraduate and/or advanced degrees, this is not compulsory for admission. Candidates who do not hold an undergraduate degree, but who have an excellent career record, examination scores and leadership potential are also welcome to apply. All those who will be completing their undergrad degree by May of the following year will be eligible to apply.

    Who are we looking for?

    We are looking for a candidate who uses available opportunities to the best of their ability, is curious to learn new things, remains open to opposing viewpoints and new ideas, and exhibits strong leadership performance and potential.

    Can applicants get a call back from Masters’ Union?

    Given the volume of requests, we’d encourage students to reach out to the admissions team via email at admissions@mastersunion.org. If students would still like to connect with us on call, reach out to our counselling team on WhatsApp using the following link.

    Can applicants defer their admission offer from Masters’ Union to the next year?

    Currently, we are not offering the option to defer admission. Those who cannot join the programme in the current year are advised to apply again next year.

    Are all applicants interviewed?

    Only candidates who have been shortlisted post their application submission will be invited for the personal interview.

    Are any of the rounds more or less competitive than others?

    Each round is equally competitive, with the same acceptance ratio across all rounds. The most important factor is to dedicate sufficient time and effort into your application to accurately showcase your strengths and talents. However, applying in Round 1 offers several advantages:

    • Scholarships: If scholarships are a priority, we encourage applying in Round 1.

    • Hostel Room Choice: Round 1 students get priority in selecting their hostel rooms, ensuring they can choose from the best available options.

    • Notice Period: They have ample time to serve notice periods at their current jobs.

    • Break Before Studies: They can take a well-deserved break before starting 16 months of intensive study.

    • Loan and Funding: They have more time to apply for loans and arrange funds.

    Can applicants get their profile evaluated?

    Feel free to connect with the admissions team to understand your fitment with the programme. While applicants are welcome to send their CV to admissions@mastersunion.org for a preliminary conversation, due to a large volume of requests, our responses might be delayed.

    Does Masters’ Union have a cut-off for MU-BAAT?

    Masters’ Union follows a holistic evaluation approach for admissions and does not have a cut-off for the MU-BAAT. Our selection process considers a combination of academic and professional experience, communication skills, and other application components.

    Do you accept GMAT and GMAT Focus Edition scores?

    Yes, we do accept both GMAT and GMAT Focus Edition scores. However, If you feel your GMAT or GMAT Focus score does not reflect your true potential, we encourage you to take the MU-BAAT to strengthen your overall application.

    Is the same reporting policy applicable to both GMAT and GMAT Focus Edition? Will this impact application?

    Yes, the same reporting policy applies to both the GMAT Focus Edition and standard GMAT. Students will have the option to report the scores of both versions of the GMAT in our application form. The choice of taking the GMAT Focus Edition will not have any negative impact on the application. Note: The conversion table designed by GMAT, is to help students convert their GMAT Focus score to the equivalent standard GMAT score and vice versa. To convert their scores, students should locate their GMAT Focus score in the left column and find the corresponding standard GMAT score in the right column.

    Are there more details about the MU-BAAT?

    Yes, the MU-BAAT (Masters' Union Business Aptitude and Admissions Test) is a key component of the admissions process. After submitting your application form, you will be required to take the MU-BAAT.

    The MU-BAAT reflects our commitment to a fair and technology-driven admissions process, focusing entirely on your critical thinking skills and potential contributions to Masters' Union. No specific preparation is needed—just show up as your authentic self.

    MU-BAAT is optional for applicants who submit a valid CAT, GMAT, or GMAT Focus score. However, if you feel your GMAT or GMAT Focus score does not reflect your true potential, we encourage you to take the MU-BAAT to strengthen your overall application.

    Application Submission Policy

    If the applicant misses all the three rounds of a particular intake, the application form and its entire content will expire. At this point, the applicant will be required to complete a new application from scratch, for the next intake. An application will only be evaluated once it is complete. If it is completed after the deadline, it will be reviewed for the next round or (in the case of last round applicants) the following intake.

    Hostel Policy

    Masters’ Union offers opt-in accommodation to students admitted to the PGP TBM. We offer a variety of options, each close to our campus and equipped with amenities to ensure a comfortable living experience. The fees for the course, as mentioned on the website, do not include hostel fees. The full fee structure, including that of the hostel, is sent to all our admitted students along with their offer letter.

    Feedback Policy

    Due to the large number of applications we receive for each intake, and the confidentiality of the deliberations of the admissions committee, we would be unable to provide detailed individual feedback on unsuccessful applications.

    Refund Policy

    Fees once submitted cannot be refunded. Masters’ Union reserves the right to amend this Refund Policy at any time without prior notice.

    Is participation in immersions mandatory or optional?

    Participation in any immersion is optional and depends on the student’s interest. Since our immersions are theme-based, students can choose the one that best aligns with their career goals and learning preferences.

    Is the immersion fee included in the tuition fee?

    The immersion fee is not included in the tuition fee. It is a separate, optional cost applicable only to students who choose to participate in the Global or Bharat Immersion programs. 1 day off campus immersions are part of your program with no added fee.

    What is the cost for participating in the immersion programs?

    The international immersions range between ₹2.5–3 lakhs, while the National Immersion is priced at approximately ₹6 lakhs for the full program. There are limited seats we encourage students to block their participation as early as possible.

    Can students choose between Global and National Immersion options?

    Yes, students can choose between Global and National Immersion options based on their interests, career goals, and preferred learning experiences.

    Are there scholarships or financial aid options available?

    Yes - we offer scholarships on a first-come, first-served basis for select immersions.

    Will there be any certification or academic recognition after completion?

    Yes! For international immersions, students receive an official certificate from the host university upon successful completion of the program. For the Bharat Immersion you will receive an official certificate from Masters’ Union.

    What kind of networking opportunities are available during the immersion?

    The immersions are designed to provide students with ample networking opportunities with faculty and peers at the host university, as well as with industry professionals and leaders during company visits and interactions.
